channel.title: physics.ed-ph updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: physics.ed-ph updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: AI and the Research-Education Environment of Physics
item.description: arXiv:2605.03185v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In the current era of AI transforming the research-education environment of physics, variety of issues and concerns arise. The KITP program "Generative AI for High and Low Energy Physics'' offered a discussion session on this, and here presented is a summary of the opinions provided in the discussion. The material is formulated such that it can serve as a starting point for further discussions in readers' research community/institution/group.
item.dc:creator: Savannah Thais, Koji Hashimoto, David S. Berman, Estelle Inack, Jessica N. Howard, Gregor Kasieczka, Aninidita Maiti, Garrett W. Merz, Javier Toledo

item.title: Study of the effect of electromagnetic damping force on a magnet oscillating near a non-ferromagnetic conducting plate
item.description: arXiv:2605.03286v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We have designed an experiment that involves studying the effects of a conducting plate on the motion of an oscillating disc magnet. We have employed the video analysis method by Tracker software to investigate the variation of electromagnetic damping coefficient with distance between the plate and the magnet. This experiment can indeed serve as a valuable educational tool for undergraduate students, covering topics such as damped oscillation, electromagnetic damping, Lenz's law, and eddy currents.
item.dc:creator: Sanjoy Kumar Pal, Soumen Sarkar, Pradipta Panchadhyayee, Debapriyo Syam

item.title: Determination of the acceleration due to gravity by studying magnet s motion through a conducting pipe
item.description: arXiv:2605.03291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We determine the acceleration due to gravity (g) in a novel way using a magnetic sensor and video analysis technique of a smartphone. The same applications are used to measure the terminal velocity of a magnet falling through a conducting pipe and the magnetic moment of the magnet from its torsional oscillations. This experiment would appear to be intriguing, as it combines elements of magnetism, terminal velocity, and electromagnetic damping to determine g.
item.dc:creator: Sanjoy Kumar Pal, Soumen Sarkar, Pradipta Panchadhyayee

item.title: A smartphone-based simple method for determination of the free space permeability
item.description: arXiv:2605.03293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A simple and novel method is designed to determine the free space permeability. This value is computed from the expression of the terminal velocity of a magnet falling through a conducting pipe using the magnetic sensor of a smartphone and a video player. This method deserves its importance because of the accuracy and precision of the results.
item.dc:creator: Sanjoy Kumar Pal, Soumen Sarkar, Pradipta Panchadhyayee
